name: Goan Style Hot and Sour Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind cumin seeds, red chilies, peppercorns, cardamom seeds, cinnamon sticks, black mustard seeds, and fenugreek seeds in a coffee grinder or other spice grinder.
2. Add the vinegar, salt, and sugar to the ground spices. Mix well and set aside.
3. Heat oil in a wide, heavy pot over medium flame. Fry onions until they turn brown and crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and transfer to an electric blender or food processor.
4. (Turn off the heat.) Add 2-3 tablespoons of water to the blender and puree the onions. Stir this into the ground spices mixture.
5. Dry pork cubes with a paper towel, removing large pieces of fat if any.
6. Blend ginger and garlic in an electric blender or food processor with 2-3 tablespoons of water until smooth.
7. Heat remaining oil in the pot over medium-high flame. Brown pork cubes in batches, then transfer to a bowl.
8. Add ginger-garlic paste to the pot, reducing heat to medium. Stir for a few seconds.
9. Add coriander and turmeric; stir again.
10. Return pork and juices to the pot with the vindaloo paste and water. Bring to a boil.
11. Cover and simmer gently until pork is tender, stirring occasionally. Serve with rice.
